title: Task graph state management with WebSocket updates
purpose: Manages task graph data fetching, real-time updates via CustomEvent, and loading/error states for React components.
module_context: Exports a hook that handles task graph state with initial fetch, event listeners, and refresh capability.

steps.content: Initialize state variables for diagram, batches, completed tasks, pending tasks, loading status, and error.
Define a callback function to fetch task graph data from the API endpoint.
Define a callback function to handle task_graph_updated CustomEvents and update state.
Set up an effect to fetch initial state and add the event listener on mount.
Return the current state and refresh function to the caller.
Set loading state to true and clear any existing error.
Construct the API URL with encoded project and session parameters.
Send a fetch request to the API endpoint.
IF the response is not ok, throw an error with status text.
Parse the JSON response from the API.
Update all state variables with data from the API response.
Set loading state to false after successful update.
IF the fetch fails, set error state and log the error to console.
Cast the event to a CustomEvent with TaskGraphUpdatedDetail payload.
Extract the payload from the event detail.
Update all state variables with data from the event payload.
Clear any existing error state after successful update.
IF parsing the event fails, log a warning to console.
Call the fetchTaskGraph function to get initial task graph data.
Add an event listener for task_graph_updated events on the window.
Return a cleanup function that removes the event listener on component unmount.
